seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Pydantic AI enables type-safe, structured data extraction from unstructured documents using schema-guided LLM calls, perfect for production-grade applications. Guardrails like Vex are essential to catch subtle failures such as schema violations, incomplete extractions, or type inconsistencies that can silently degrade data quality. Integrating Vex as a result validator ensures reliable outputs even under edge cases common in document processing.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with document ingestion (PDFs, text files) feeding into a Pydantic AI agent configured with Pydantic models defining the target schema (e.g., Invoice, Resume). The agent uses an LLM provider like OpenAI with structured output mode, guided by the schema to extract fields like dates, amounts, and entities. Post-extraction, result validators (now enhanced with Vex) enforce completeness, accuracy, and type safety before data flows to downstream systems like databases or analytics pipelines.

Key components include the AgentRunner for orchestration, Tool integrations for multi-step extraction, and streaming support for large documents. Nested tool calls handle complex logic like table parsing, while dependency injection manages context like document metadata across calls. Vex slots in as a drop-in result_validator to monitor and retry failed extractions transparently.

risks.name: Result validator rejecting valid but unexpected model outputs
risks.description: LLM generates semantically correct data in unexpected formats (e.g., 'N/A' instead of null for missing fields)
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic retry with flexible schema guards

risks.name: Tool retry logic masking persistent underlying errors
risks.description: Document parsing tools fail repeatedly on malformed PDFs but retries hide the root cause

risks.name: Dependency injection context not available in nested tool calls
risks.description: Nested extraction loses document page context, causing incomplete field population
risks.mitigation: Vex context preservation across agent calls

risks.name: Streaming structured responses failing mid-generation validation
risks.description: Large document extraction streams partial valid JSON that fails final schema check
risks.mitigation: Vex streaming validation with checkpoint recovery

implementation.title: Define Extraction Schema with Pydantic
implementation.description: Create Pydantic models for your target document structure
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, List

class Invoice(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str = Field(..., description="Unique invoice ID")
    date: str = Field(..., description="Invoice date in YYYY-MM-DD")
    total_amount: float = Field(..., ge=0)
    line_items: List[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)
    vendor: Optional[str] = None

implementation.title: Create Pydantic AI Agent
implementation.description: Configure agent with schema and LLM provider
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, AgentRunner
from pydantic_ai.providers.openai import OpenAIProvider

llm = OpenAIProvider(model="gpt-4o")
agent = Agent(
    model=llm,
    model_kwargs={"response_format": {"type": "json_schema", "json_schema": Invoice.model_json_schema()}},
    result_validator=lambda x: Invoice.model_validate(x)  # Vex will replace this
)
runner = AgentRunner(agents=[agent])

implementation.title: Basic Document Extraction
implementation.code:
document_text = "Invoice #INV-1234 dated 2024-03-15. Total: $1,250. Vendor: Acme Corp"

result = runner.run_sync(
    "Extract invoice data from this document:", 
    context=[{"role": "user", "content": document_text}]
)

invoice: Invoice = result.final_output
print(f"Invoice #{invoice.invoice_number}, Total: ${invoice.total_amount}")

implementation.title: Add Tool for Complex Extraction
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import tool

@tool
async def extract_tables(text: str) -> List[dict]:
    """Extract tables from document text"""
    return []  # LLM-powered table extraction

agent = Agent(
    model=llm,
    tools=[extract_tables],
    result_validator=lambda x: Invoice.model_validate(x)
)

vexIntegration.title: Install and Initialize Vex SDK
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex to your Pydantic AI project
vexIntegration.code:
pip install pydantic-ai vex-sdk

from vex import Vex, Guardrail
from vex.guardrails import SchemaCompliance, FieldAccuracy, Completeness

# Initialize Vex client
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

# Define extraction guardrail
guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="invoice-extraction",
    verifications=[
        SchemaCompliance(),
        FieldAccuracy(fields=["total_amount", "date"]),
        Completeness(),
    ]
)
vex.guardrails.create(guardrail)

vexIntegration.title: Replace Result Validator with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Swap Pydantic's validator with Vex integration
vexIntegration.code:
from vex.integrations.pydantic_ai import VexResultValidator

# Replace basic validator with Vex
agent = Agent(
    model=llm,
    model_kwargs={"response_format": {"type": "json_schema", "json_schema": Invoice.model_json_schema()}},
    result_validator=VexResultValidator(
        guardrail_id="invoice-extraction",
        vex_client=vex,
        max_retries=3
    )
)

vexIntegration.title: Run Production Extraction with Vex Monitoring
vexIntegration.description: Execute with full Vex observability and auto-recovery
vexIntegration.code:
def extract_invoice(document_text: str) -> Invoice:
    with vex.span(name="invoice-extraction"):
        result = runner.run_sync(
            "Extract invoice data:",
            context=[{"role": "user", "content": document_text}]
        )
        return result.final_output

# Production usage
invoice = extract_invoice(large_document_text)
print(vex.get_recent_failures(guardrail_id="invoice-extraction"))
